title: The Top 5 Scariest Summer Movies and ‘Talk to Me’
format: Sean reviews the Australian horror hit Talk to Me, counts down his five favorite scary summer movies, then talks to directors Danny and Michael Philippou about their YouTube-to-Sundance path, and to Christian Petzold about his new film Afire.

films.title: Talk to Me
films.note: Directed by Danny and Michael Philippou, starring Sophie Wilde, Alexandra Jensen, Joe Bird, and Otis Dhanji. A group of friends conjure spirits with an embalmed hand until one session goes too far. Sean calls it a horror MacGuffin movie in the vein of The Ring or Hellraiser, with a breakout performance from Wilde.

segments.heading: Scary Summer Movie Tropes
segments.note: cursed objects and cabins in the woods

films.title: The Ring
films.note: The cursed videotape as horror MacGuffin.

films.title: Child's Play
films.note: Chucky as horror MacGuffin.

films.title: The Evil Dead
films.note: The Necronomicon as horror MacGuffin.

films.title: Christine
films.note: John Carpenter's Stephen King adaptation, about the possessed 1958 Plymouth Fury.

films.title: Hellraiser
films.note: The Lament Configuration puzzle box. Sean thinks it'd make a good double feature with Talk to Me.

films.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
films.note: The Kevin Williamson post-Scream boom.

films.title: Friday the 13th
films.note: The classic summer camp trope.

films.title: Straw Dogs
films.note: Sam Peckinpah's country-house-invaders picture. Not pure horror, Sean says, but a similar shape.

films.title: The Cabin in the Woods
films.note: One of Sean's favorite genre satires of the 2010s.

films.title: Us
films.note: Jordan Peele's film, which Sean thinks also fits the cabin-in-the-woods mold.

films.title: The Blackening
films.note: Sean and Amanda covered this horror-comedy on a recent episode.

films.title: The Texas Chain Saw Massacre
films.note: No. 1: one of the most important movies ever made, in Sean's view. Most horror since is riffing on it in some way.

films.title: It Follows
films.note: No. 2: David Robert Mitchell's STD parable. Still genuinely scary, with several truly chilling moments.

films.title: The Hills Have Eyes
films.note: No. 3: one of Wes Craven's first films. A family road trip gone terribly wrong that Sean says plays differently after Oppenheimer.

films.title: Sleepaway Camp
films.note: No. 4: 40 years old and still, per Sean, best known for a genuinely shocking ending.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: No. 5: Ari Aster's sunlit pagan-cult film, which Sean calls probably the single brightest horror movie ever made, an interesting contrast to Aster's own Hereditary.

films.title: Talk to Me
films.note: The brothers' debut feature, born out of the RackaRacka YouTube channel, picked up by A24 out of Sundance.

films.title: Project A
films.note: Michael's last-great-thing pick: a Jackie Chan action film he loves for the rawness and danger of the stunt work.

films.title: Beasts of No Nation
films.note: Danny's last-great-thing pick: he keeps returning to the scene of child soldiers marching into battle singing.

films.title: Afire
films.note: Petzold's new film, referred to on the show as 'A Fire': a summer-house portrait of a novelist. Written after Petzold caught COVID in Paris and binged Eric Rohmer movies while sick.

films.title: People on Sunday
films.note: The German silent Petzold cites as the model summer movie, directed by Billy Wilder, Fred Zinnemann, Edgar Ulmer, and Robert Siodmak, all of whom later fled for Hollywood.

films.title: Summer with Monika
films.note: Petzold's example of a Swedish summer movie, from Ingmar Bergman.

films.title: What's Up, Doc?
films.note: Peter Bogdanovich screwball comedy Petzold showed his cast during rehearsals.

films.title: Some Came Running
films.note: Another film Petzold screened for the cast before shooting.

films.title: The Shining
films.note: Petzold points to a tennis-ball-against-the-wall echo of Jack Nicholson's Jack Torrance.

films.title: Phoenix
films.note: Sean calls this a personal favorite among Petzold's films.

films.title: Transit
films.note: Where Petzold first met Paula Beer, through their shared casting agent on François Ozon's Frantz.

films.title: Barbara
films.note: Sean mentions rewatching this Petzold film the night before the interview.

films.title: Undine
films.note: The first film in what Petzold jokingly calls his 'Elements trilogy,' also with Paula Beer.

films.title: Frantz
films.note: François Ozon's film, on which Petzold worked on the German dialogue; he met Paula Beer through it.

films.title: Ticket to Paradise
films.note: The Julia Roberts/George Clooney comedy Petzold watched on a plane and did not care for.

films.title: The Quiet Girl
films.note: Petzold's last-great-thing pick: an Irish, Oscar-nominated film he went into cold.

I Know What You Did Last Summer.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
